sources: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Final Advance Appropriation)

category_1.name: Trust Fund Excise Taxes and Fees

category_2.name: Grants-in-Aid for Airports (AIP and Terminal Programs)

2026.methodology: The 2026 forecast reflects the $22.0B base budget request combined with the final $5.0B installment from the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA). Income distribution is modeled on projected 4.9% passenger traffic growth (RPK) increasing trust fund receipts. Spending prioritizes a 'down payment' of $450M for multi-year radar replacement and funding for 2,500 new air traffic controllers.

sources: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Advance Appropriations)

category_2.name: Grants-in-Aid for Airports (AIP and BIL Grants)

2025.methodology: The 2025 figures represent the total budgetary resources including $21.9B in base discretionary request and $5.0B in mandatory BIL funding. Income calculation accounts for increased Trust Fund receipts due to air traffic volume growth. Spending reflects the strategic shift towards workforce expansion (ATC hiring) and NAS infrastructure replacement.

sources: General Fund of the U.S. Government
Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Bipartisan Infrastructure Law)

category_1.name: Airport and Airway Trust Fund Excise Taxes

2024.methodology: Data derived from the FY 2024 Enacted Budget and DOT Budget Highlights. Total budgetary resources combine the base discretionary appropriations ($19.8B) and the mandatory advance appropriations from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law ($5.0B). Income is equated to Budget Authority as per government accounting standards for federal agencies.
